title: A Canker In The Heart
content:
I made a bitter song
    When I was a boy,
    About a girl
    With hot earth-coloured hair,
    Who lived with me
    And left me.

    I made a sour song
    On her marriage-day,
    That ever his kisses
    Would be ghosts of mine,
    And ever the measure
    Of his halting love
    Flow to my music.

    It was a silly song,
    Dear wife with cool black hair,
    And yet when I recall
    (At night with you asleep)
    That once you gave yourself
    Before we met,
    I do not quite well know
    What song to make.

    From the Burmese (nineteenth century) (� by Asmapur).
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator) delivers a powerful performance in "A Canker In The Heart"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
